title: `podman-4.1.1` rejects one of only two non-alphanumeric Label characters Docker accepts as valid
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

In the [Docker documentation](https://docs.docker.com/config/labels-custom-metadata/), it is stated that the only valid (but currently unenforced) Label format is to start and end with a lower-case letter and contain only lower-case letters, numbers, and periods or hyphens.

On reading this, I updated my labels to fulfil the lower-case requirement and changed underscores for hypens.

However, the output I then get from `podman` is:

```
$ sudo podman image inspect '--format={{.Config.Labels.entrypoint-sum}}' localhost/init:latest
ERRO[0000] Printing inspect output: template: image inspect:1: bad character U+002D '-'
```

… now I understand that this project may not wish to be as opinionated as the Docker project as to the formatting of labels (especially when this is unenforced), but it would be beneficial to at least support the format which Docker has settled on?

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a Container image with a Label in a Docker-recommended format (containing a hyphen);

2. Attempt to `inspect` the image to retrieve the specific label (only).

**Describe the results you received:**

```
ERRO[0000] Printing inspect output: template: image inspect:1: bad character U+002D '-'
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

The label contents to by printed, e.g.

```
$ sudo podman image inspect '--format={{.Config.Labels.maintainer}}' localhost/init:latest
xxx@xxx.me
```

(This being the same image: Labels without a hyphen do work)

Additionally, there should not be a discrepancy between what can be added as a Label and what can be retrieved… unless this is a Go command-line escaping issue, or similar?

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 4.1.1
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


Yes

comments.body:
This is not valid go template syntax, I would be very surprised if this works with docker.

https://pkg.go.dev/text/template

> The name of a key of the data, which must be a map, preceded by a period, such as .Key The result is the map element value indexed by the key. Key invocations may be chained and combined with fields to any depth: .Field1.Key1.Field2.Key2 Although the key must be an alphanumeric identifier, unlike with field names they do not need to start with an upper case letter. Keys can also be evaluated on variables, including chaining: $x.key1.key2

You have to use the index function:
`{{index .Config.Labels "entrypoint-sum"}}`

> This is not valid go template syntax
> 
> You have to use the index function: `{{index .Config.Labels "entrypoint-sum"}}`

This would be great to add to the `podman inspect` man-page, which currently says only:

> The keys of the returned JSON can be used as the values for the --format flag (see examples below).

… with the examples not including anything more complex such as the syntax you’ve suggested!
